Special Corrsspo idencs to The Press


PITTSBURG, Pa, Nov. To-
Twenty thousand women will
march on Harrisburg, the capital of
Pennsylvania, in January, in an eff
fort to induce the legislature to
pass a local option bill. The Penn
sylvania Women's Christian Tenn
perance union hopes by this
method to place the Keystone state
in the list OF dry" commonwealth.
The decision to storm the next
legislature has quickly followed the
return of the Pennsylvania delega-
ton from the national convention
of the w. c. T. u. in Colorado. At
this convention large map of the
United States was suspended from
the ceiling, the dry staes being
printed in white and those permit
ting the sale of liquor being in
black.


And Pennsylvania was the
blacklist of the black" Indignantly
declares Mrs. j. B. Masters, wife of
a local attorney, who will lead the
Pittsburg women at Harrisburg.
and the best known local temper
ance worker.


When Mrs. Masters becomes
identified with any movement is us
ually is attended with success and
spectacular features. 1t was she
who led the fight against five of the
best known Pittsburg cafe keepers,
and had their licenses revoked be-
cause they sold liquor to young
girls. She conducted this crusade
by going personally into the gilded
dining rooms and demonstrating
with girls who were drinking there
with their escorts.


Mrs, ella M, George of Beaver
Falls, state president of the w. G.
r. t.. has issued the call for the
10000 women to beard the Pennsyl-
vania solons In their den This was
decided on after the Keystone state
women, who had to hang their
heads In shame at the convention
because of the poor showing made
by Pennsylvania. determined that
heroic measures were necessary to
save the state.
